_source: ASE Certification Study Guides, Mitchell1 diagnostic procedures, OEM service manuals
_note: Long-tail automotive diagnostics. Specific failure modes, sensor specs, OBD-II codes interpretation.

second_character.0: Generic SAE code (same across all manufacturers)

P0300.diagnosis: Check freeze frame data; identify which cylinders; compression test; spark test; fuel pressure test

causes: Vacuum leak (intake manifold, brake booster, PCV)
P0171_P0174.diagnosis: Smoke test for vacuum leaks; clean MAF with MAF cleaner; check fuel pressure (should be 40-60 PSI most vehicles); check fuel trims (LTFT/STFT)

P0420_P0430.diagnosis: Check upstream/downstream O2 sensor switching; downstream should be flat ~0.5V; if switching like upstream, cat failed; also check for rattling (broken substrate)

P0128.diagnosis: Check coolant temp reaches 195-220°F; if stays <180°F, thermostat stuck; IR temp gun on upper radiator hose

causes: Missing or loose gas cap (80% of cases)
P0455.diagnosis: Tighten/replace gas cap; clear code; if returns, smoke test EVAP system; check purge valve operation

fuel_trims.stft_short_term: Real-time correction; -10% to +10% normal; >±10% indicates problem being actively corrected
fuel_trims.ltft_long_term: Learned average correction; -10% to +10% normal; represents chronic condition
fuel_trims.positive_trim: +15% LTFT = adding 15% more fuel = lean condition (vacuum leak, weak pump, clogged filter)
fuel_trims.negative_trim: -15% LTFT = subtracting fuel = rich condition (leaking injector, bad MAF, bad O2 sensor)
fuel_trims.combined_trim: STFT + LTFT; if combined >±25%, drivability issues likely; investigate cause

maf_sensor.function: Measures air mass entering engine; PCM uses to calculate fuel
maf_sensor.testing: Key on engine off: 0-0.1V; idle (warm): 0.6-1.2V; 2500 RPM: 2.0-3.0V; readings in grams/sec on scan tool
failure_modes: Contaminated (oil, dirt) reads low → rich mixture, black smoke
Failed reads 0V → default fuel map, poor performance
maf_sensor.cleaning: MAF-specific cleaner only; do not touch sensor wire; 2 sprays; let dry; never use throttle body cleaner or carb cleaner

upstream_narrowband.switching_rate: Should switch lean/rich 1-3x per second at idle; slow switching = lazy sensor
upstream_narrowband.lifespan: 60-100k miles; contamination from oil burning or coolant leak kills sensor

downstream_narrowband.voltage: Should be stable ~0.5V; if switching like upstream, cat failed
downstream_narrowband.purpose: Monitor catalyst efficiency; not used for fuel control

wideband_afr.voltage: Linear output; 0-5V or current output; measures exact AFR from 10:1 to 20:1
wideband_afr.used_in: Bank 1 sensor 1 on newer vehicles (2010+); more precise fuel control

coolant_temp_sensor.resistance: Negative temperature coefficient (NTC); resistance decreases as temp increases
coolant_temp_sensor.testing: Unplug connector; measure resistance across sensor terminals; compare to chart; out of range = bad sensor
coolant_temp_sensor.symptoms_if_failed: Hard start when cold (reads hot, too little fuel); poor fuel economy (reads cold, too much fuel)

throttle_position_sensor.voltage: Closed throttle: 0.5-1.0V; wide open: 4.0-5.0V; should sweep smoothly
throttle_position_sensor.testing: Scan tool graphing or multimeter; slowly open throttle; watch for jumps or dead spots
throttle_position_sensor.failure: Stumble or hesitation at certain throttle positions; check voltage sweep for glitches

crankshaft_position_sensor.function: Tells PCM engine RPM and piston position; required for fuel and spark timing
crankshaft_position_sensor.testing: AC voltage test: crank engine, should see 0.3-1.0V AC; or scope pattern shows pulses
crankshaft_position_sensor.symptoms: No start or stalling; PCM needs crank signal to fire injectors and coils
crankshaft_position_sensor.common_failure: Heat-related; works when cold, fails when hot; repl sensor and check air gap (0.020-0.050")

coil_on_plug.design: Individual coil per cylinder; no distributor
coil_on_plug.testing: Swap coils between cylinders; if misfire moves to new cylinder, coil bad
coil_on_plug.resistance: Primary: 0.5-2.0 ohms; secondary: 5k-15k ohms (varies by design)
coil_on_plug.failure_mode: Misfire on one cylinder; code P030X where X is cylinder number

spark_plugs.gap_specification: 0.035-0.060" typical; wider gap for more power, requires stronger coil

ignition_timing.base_timing: Timing with advance disconnected; typically 6-10° BTDC at idle
ignition_timing.total_advance: Base + mechanical + vacuum; ~32-36° at 2500 RPM cruise
ignition_timing.check_with_timing_light: Warm engine; disconnect vacuum advance; check marks align with spec; reconnect vacuum; should advance 10-15°

fuel_pressure_specs.port_fuel_injection: 40-60 PSI typical; 35-45 PSI GM, 55-65 PSI Ford/Chrysler
fuel_pressure_specs.direct_injection: 2000-3000 PSI at high-pressure pump; 50-60 PSI at low-pressure pump
fuel_pressure_specs.testing: Install fuel pressure gauge; key on engine off (KOEO); should meet spec; start engine, should hold; load engine, should not drop >5 PSI

fuel_pump_testing.current_draw: 4-8A typical for in-tank pump; >10A indicates pump laboring (clogged filter or failing pump)
fuel_pump_testing.pressure_decay: Turn off engine; pressure should hold >30 PSI for 5 minutes; rapid drop = leaking injector or check valve
fuel_pump_testing.volume_test: Disconnect return line; run pump; should flow 1 pint in 30 seconds; low volume = clogged strainer or weak pump

injector_testing.resistance: 12-16 ohms typical for saturated (high-impedance); 2-5 ohms for peak-and-hold (low-impedance)
injector_testing.flow_test: Remove injectors; pulse with power supply; collect fuel in graduated cylinders; all should flow within 5% of each other
injector_testing.balance_test: Run engine; disable one injector at a time; RPM drop should be similar for all cylinders; low drop = clogged injector or bad cylinder
injector_testing.cleaning: On-car: pressurized cleaner through fuel rail; off-car: ultrasonic cleaning; replace if >100k miles and clogged

battery_testing.load_test: Load to half CCA rating for 15 seconds; voltage should stay >9.6V; <9.6V = bad battery
battery_testing.parasitic_draw: Acceptable: <50mA (0.05A); disconnect negative cable; insert ammeter; pull fuses one-by-one to isolate circuit

alternator_testing.output_voltage: 13.8-14.4V at idle with accessories on; <13.5V = undercharge; >15V = overcharge (bad regulator)
alternator_testing.output_current: Load alternator with headlights, blower, heated seats; should maintain voltage and provide rated amps (80-150A typical)
alternator_testing.ripple_test: AC voltage across battery; should be <0.5V AC; >0.5V indicates bad diodes in alternator

starter_testing.voltage_drop: Positive side: <0.5V drop from battery + to starter +; negative side: <0.5V drop from battery - to starter case
starter_testing.current_draw: 150-250A typical for 4-cyl; 250-350A for V8; >400A indicates high resistance (bad starter or engine mechanical issue)
starter_testing.bench_test: Remove starter; apply 12V directly; should spin fast and draw <100A no-load

fluid_condition.color: Red or pink = good; brown = oxidized (change fluid); black/burnt smell = severe damage
fluid_condition.level: Check hot in park (most vehicles); low level = slipping, burnt fluid; high level = foaming, erratic shifts
fluid_condition.metal_particles: Small amount on magnet normal; chunks or glitter = internal wear; drain and inspect pan

shift_problems.no_upshift: Check governor pressure sensor; check shift solenoids; check throttle position sensor; TCM may need reprogramming
shift_problems.harsh_shifts: High line pressure; bad pressure control solenoid; worn clutches; TCM adaptive learning may help
shift_problems.slipping: Low fluid level first; then worn clutches or bands; pressure test required; rebuild likely
shift_problems.delayed_engagement: Worn clutch packs; low line pressure; cold weather thickens fluid (normal if <5 sec delay when cold)

abs_diagnostics.wheel_speed_sensors: AC voltage signal; test by spinning wheel, should see 0.5V+ AC; also check reluctor ring for damage
abs_diagnostics.abs_codes: C0035-C0050 typically wheel speed sensors; compare all four sensors at same speed; mismatched = bad sensor
abs_diagnostics.bleeding_abs: Some systems require scan tool to cycle ABS pump; traditional bleeding won't remove air from ABS module

brake_noise_diagnosis.squealing: Worn pads (wear indicator tab touching rotor); glazed pads; lack of anti-squeal paste
brake_noise_diagnosis.grinding: Metal-to-metal; pads worn through; immediate replacement; likely rotor damage
brake_noise_diagnosis.pulsation: Warped rotors; check runout with dial indicator; >0.003" = resurface or replace
brake_noise_diagnosis.pulling: Uneven pad wear; seized caliper; contaminated pad (oil/grease); air in one side of system
